page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.large is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, corresponding to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance is positioned at the lower end of the compute spectrum. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the bottom 25 percent, while multi-core metrics drop to the bottom 10 percent, reflecting the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop-off from cached small-block reads at 158.75 GB/sec to uncached large-block reads at 16.93 GB/sec and writes at 13.99 GB/sec. Cryptographic and decompression tasks, such as Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, also register in the bottom 10 percent of tested hardware.

Given the shared CPU resources and lack of local storage, the ecs.mn4.large is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads. These include basic static web serving, small-scale Redis caching, development environments, and simple utility scripts. The instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for resource-light applications that do not require sustained high-performance compute or high-speed network throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.large is a general-purpose, entry-level instance featuring 2 shared vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, this x86_64 instance is designed for resource-light applications. Benchmark data places its single-core and multi-core compute performance in the lower percentiles, with stress-ng and Geekbench results reflecting its shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth also shows a steep decline when handling uncached large blocks. Consequently, this instance is highly cost-efficient for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, development and testing environments, small Redis caches, and simple background utility tasks that do not demand high-throughput networking or sustained, intensive CPU utilization.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.large is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight workloads and development environments.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-CPU virtual instance with balanced memory for lightweight development, testing, and basic web serving workloads.

No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Suited for lightweight static web serving and small Redis caches
Cost-efficient option for non-critical development and testing
